payload.title: Gravity - Fundamental Force
payload.text: Gravity is a fundamental force that attracts objects with mass toward each other. It is one of the four fundamental forces in nature, along with electromagnetism, weak nuclear force, and strong nuclear force. Newton's law of universal gravitation describes the attractive force between two objects with mass.
payload.summary: Gravity is a fundamental force that attracts objects with mass.

payload.title: Quantum Physics - Subatomic World
payload.text: Quantum physics is the branch of physics that studies matter and energy at the smallest scales. It describes the behavior of particles at the atomic and subatomic level, where classical physics no longer applies. Key concepts include wave-particle duality, uncertainty principle, and quantum entanglement.
payload.summary: Quantum physics studies matter and energy at atomic and subatomic scales.

payload.title: Cold War - Historical Period
payload.text: The Cold War was a period of geopolitical tension between the Soviet Union and the United States and their respective allies, the Eastern Bloc and the Western Bloc, after World War II. It lasted from 1947 to 1991 and was characterized by proxy wars, nuclear arms race, and ideological conflict.
payload.summary: The Cold War was a period of geopolitical tension after World War II.

payload.title: Inflation - Economic Concept
payload.text: Inflation is the rate at which the general level of prices for goods and services rises, eroding purchasing power. Central banks attempt to limit inflation and avoid deflation to keep the economy running smoothly. Moderate inflation is generally considered healthy for economic growth.
payload.summary: Inflation is the rate at which prices for goods and services rise.

payload.title: Latitude - Geographic Coordinate
payload.text: Latitude is a geographic coordinate that specifies the north-south position of a point on the Earth's surface. Latitude is an angle which ranges from 0° at the Equator to 90° at the poles. Lines of latitude run parallel to the equator and are used together with longitude to locate any point on Earth.
payload.summary: Latitude is a geographic coordinate specifying north-south position.

payload.title: Electricity - Electrical Energy
payload.text: Electricity is the set of physical phenomena associated with the presence and motion of matter that has a property of electric charge. It is related to magnetism and both are part of the phenomenon of electromagnetism. Electricity powers modern civilization through electrical grids.
payload.summary: Electricity is the physical phenomena associated with electric charge.

payload.title: Transformer - Attention Mechanism
payload.text: The transformer is a deep learning architecture introduced in the paper 'Attention Is All You Need'. It relies entirely on self-attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely. Transformers have become the foundation of modern NLP models.
payload.summary: The transformer is a deep learning architecture based on attention mechanisms.
